intro: Freirea serbica, also known as Balkan maple, is a deciduous tree species native to the Balkan Peninsula. The health benefits of Freirea serbica are attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Freirea serbica is often used in traditional medicine and is commonly prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or decoction to take advantage of its health benefits.
